parsed_findings.chain_steps: SSRF → cloud metadata → IAM credentials → privilege escalation

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Open redirect abuse → internal service access → data exfiltration
parsed_findings.impact: Allowlist bypass enabling access to internal services not reachable directly.
parsed_findings.proof: GET /fetch?url=http://attacker.com/redirect → 302 → http://internal-service/ followed by axios transparently.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Host a redirect from an allowlisted-looking domain to an internal target; axios follows by default.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Unvalidated URL parameter passed directly to axios; even a repaired allowlist can be bypassed via redirects or DNS rebinding since no redirect policy is set.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1) Enumerate invoice IDs via sequential int or IDOR. 2) PATCH with attacker session. 3) Confirm note update on victim invoice.
parsed_findings.impact: Unauthorized modification of another user's invoice notes if authorization logic has a gap or inv.project_id defaults permissively.
parsed_findings.proof: GET /api/invoices/999 returns inv object before user_can_edit is called; if inv.project_id is null/default, the check may pass.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send PATCH /api/invoices/<victim_id> with a valid session; observe if 403 is returned or if note is updated on an invoice belonging to another user.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: IDOR: inv_id is fetched before authorization; if db.get_invoice raises no error on missing/other-tenant invoices, the 403 check may be bypassable or leak existence.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1) Inject payload via PATCH. 2) Trigger victim to view invoice. 3) XSS executes in victim browser.
parsed_findings.impact: Stored XSS affecting any user viewing the invoice if the frontend renders the note field without escaping.
parsed_findings.proof: PATCH body {"note":"<script>alert(1)</script>"} is stored verbatim via inv.note assignment with no sanitization.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: 1) Send PATCH with XSS payload in note field. 2) View invoice in frontend. 3) Confirm script execution if output is rendered as raw HTML.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: No re-validation of note content allows mass-assignment or stored XSS if inv.to_json() is rendered as HTML without escaping.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1) Login as normal user 2) PATCH own id with elevated role fields 3) Re-authenticate to confirm privilege escalation
parsed_findings.impact: Full privilege escalation; attacker can promote themselves to admin or support role.
parsed_findings.proof: Object.assign(user, req.body) merges all attacker-controlled body fields onto the user model with no allowlist.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: PATCH /api/users/:id with body {"isAdmin":true,"isSupport":true} to escalate privileges.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Mass assignment via Object.assign allows overwriting any user field including role/isAdmin/isSupport from req.body.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1) Obtain support account 2) PATCH arbitrary user id 3) Combine with CWE-915 to escalate victim to admin
parsed_findings.impact: Horizontal/vertical privilege escalation; support accounts can overwrite any user record.
parsed_findings.proof: Condition `req.user.id req.user.isSupport` is syntactically broken; intended logic is ambiguous but isSupport alone may bypass the id check.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: As a support user, PATCH /api/users/<victim-id> — if condition short-circuits on isSupport, the 403 is never returned.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Authorization check uses || instead of && (likely syntax bug), so any support user can modify any other user's account.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1) Send PATCH with random UUID 2) Observe 500 response body for stack/db info
parsed_findings.impact: Information disclosure of server internals; may also cause denial-of-service via repeated invalid IDs.
parsed_findings.proof: db.users.findById may return null for unknown IDs; no null guard before Object.assign causes unhandled exception.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: PATCH /api/users/nonexistent-id — server throws TypeError, potentially exposing internal stack trace in error response.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: No existence check after findById; if user is null, Object.assign(null, req.body) throws and may leak stack trace.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Exploit race -> negative balance -> chain to withdrawal endpoint for fund extraction
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker drains account below zero, effectively stealing funds or creating unbounded credit.
parsed_findings.proof: Two concurrent POST /transfer requests both read src before either write commits, bypassing the insufficient funds check.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send two simultaneous requests with amt=full_balance; both pass line 6 check and both debit, resulting in negative balance.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Race condition (TOCTOU) in balance check-then-update allows double-spend.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Negative amount bypass -> self-credit inflation -> withdraw inflated balance
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can arbitrarily inflate their own balance or drain victim accounts with a negative amount.
parsed_findings.proof: int(request.json['amount']) with amt=-100 passes src < amt check (e.g. 0 < -100 is false only if src>=0, but negative amt causes set_balance to ADD funds to sender).
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST /transfer {"to":"attacker","amount":"-500"}; src-(-500) increases sender balance while victim receives -500 deducted.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: No input validation on 'to' or 'amount' fields; negative amount enables reverse transfer (credit self).

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Phantom account creation -> transfer funds -> register account post-transfer to claim balance
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can pre-create phantom accounts or cause accounting inconsistencies leading to fund loss from the system.
parsed_findings.proof: db.set_balance(to, db.get_balance(to)+amt) will upsert balance for any arbitrary 'to' string without existence check.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: No validation that 'to' is a valid/existing user; funds sent to nonexistent account are created out of thin air.
